mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 100k jobs signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. This drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations reflect stronger earnings expectations from consumer-facing sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 payroll level 130,539k indicates post-recession recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% for three consecutive months, the zero-lower-bound regime signals persistent monetary accommodation. This depresses risk-free rates and forces capital into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 FEDFUNDS at 0.19% confirms ZLB regime active
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 5d forward returns becomes positive (opposite of predicted direction).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand. This triggers equity market repricing downward as forward earnings estimates decline. The mechanism operates over 60 trading days as the demand shock propagates through supply chains and corporate guidance revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:October 2010 HOUST at 519k and RSAFS at 378.7B show post-crisis stabilization
regime_hint: late-cycle demand weakness or early-stage contraction
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the joint demand contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month indicates accelerating real economic capacity utilization. This signals pricing power and margin expansion for equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors revise earnings forecasts upward.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 INDPRO at 93.90 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20d forward returns following INDPRO > 1% MoM growth are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress due to lower earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 UNRATE at 9.8% reflects elevated post-crisis unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising core inflation (CPIAUCSL) and accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS near zero) creates real rate compression. This interaction forces equity risk premiums lower and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real asset valuations rise relative to nominal bonds.
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 CPIAUCSL at 219.15 and FEDFUNDS at 0.19% show negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2% annualized rate signals robust aggregate demand expansion. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth accelerates and equity risk premiums compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2010 GDPC1 at 13,277.4B indicates post-recession recovery underway
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, it signals stagflationary pressure. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations face dual compression from lower growth and higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE increases > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:November 2010 INDPRO at 93.90 and UNRATE at 9.8% show post-crisis stabilization without stagflation
regime_hint: stagflationary regime or supply shock with labor market weakness
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the stagflation signal.
